description_long: This object consists of multiple brown cardboard storage boxes, each with flaps in various open and closed positions. The boxes are rectangular and feature visible tape strips sealing some of the lids, while others are partially or fully open, revealing empty interiors. The surfaces are textured with a slightly worn, corrugated appearance, and some boxes display faint printed markings or symbols typical of shipping containers. The boxes are arranged in neat rows, suggesting they are ready for packing, storage, or transport. There are no visible handles or locking mechanisms, and the boxes rest directly on the ground. The overall look is utilitarian, suitable for use in warehouses, garages, or urban storage environments.
description: A group of brown cardboard storage boxes with open and closed flaps, tape strips, and worn surfaces, arranged in rows for storage or shipping.
description_view_0: Front view showing open flaps and the interior of several boxes, with tape strips visible on some lids.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rectangular shape and depth of the boxes, with some flaps fully closed and others open.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the arrangement of boxes and the open interiors, as well as the distribution of tape and flap positions.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the backs and sides of the boxes, with closed and partially open flaps visible.
